title: 10 best herbal tinctures for neck pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and discomfort in the muscles and joints. The herb's sedative qualities also help to relax tense neck muscles, easing stiffness and rigidity. Additionally, valerian's analgesic effects block pain signals to the brain, providing relief from aching and numbness. By promoting deep relaxation and reducing inflammation, herbal valerian tinctures provide natural and effective relief from chronic neck pain, allowing for improved mobility and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Valerian's sedative and relaxing effects can help to calm muscle spasms and tension in the neck, reducing pain and stiffness.
Analgesic: Valerian contains compounds that have analgesic properties, which can help to reduce or eliminate pain in the neck caused by inflammation, strain, or injury.
Anti-inflammatory: Valerian's anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck, which can contribute to chronic pain and stiffness.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: It helps with neck pain by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which promotes relaxation, reduces muscle spasms, and eases pain.
Isovaleric acid: It contributes to the analgesic properties of Valeriana officinalis by acting as a potent pain reliever, potentially through the inhibition of inflammatory responses.
Valeranone: It has sedative properties and can help alleviate neck pain by promoting relaxation, reducing muscle tension, and improving sleep quality.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian root is commonly used due to its high concentration of valerenic acid, a compound with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve neck pain.

Stems: Valerian stems are also used, as they contain a similar range of bioactive compounds as the roots, including valepotriates and isovaleric acid, which contribute to their pain-relieving effects.

Leaves: Valerian leaves are occasionally used, although to a lesser extent than roots or stems, as they still contain some of the plant's medicinal compounds, such as valerenic acid and other sesquiterpenes, which may help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots and stems in quantities of 250 grams for a standard batch.
Chop the valeriana roots and stems into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped valeriana pieces with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to allow the extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which can effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area. The tincture's flavonoids and glycosides work together to relax muscles and joints, alleviating tension that may be causing stiffness and soreness. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have analgesic properties, helping to numb the pain and promote a sense of relaxation and calm. By addressing both inflammation and pain, herbal licorice tinctures can provide natural relief for neck pain sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Herbal licorice tincture contains glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the muscles and joints of the neck, alleviating pain and discomfort.

Pain-relieving: Licorice root extract has been shown to have analgesic and anesthetic properties, which can help numb and reduce pain in the neck region, making it a natural alternative to over-the-counter pain relievers.

Muscle relaxant: The flavonoids present in licorice tincture have been found to have a calming effect on muscle spasms and cramps, helping to relieve tension and stiffness in the neck muscles, which can contribute to neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's response to stress, which can contribute to neck pain.
Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin): Contributes to neck pain relief by acting as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain in the affected area.
Licoricidin (an isoflavonoid): Has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing pain perception and inflammation in the neck muscles and joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used to make tinctures for neck pain as they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain.
Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are also used to make tinctures for neck pain as they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra can be used to make tinctures for neck pain as they contain saponins and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots with a thickness of 2-3mm for optimal medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 100g of the powdered root with 500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass container for 2 weeks.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place at room temperature for 14 days to allow for maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marijuana tinctures helps with neck pain because they contain cannabinoids that interact with the body's endocannabinoid system, which plays a crucial role in regulating pain perception. The CBD and THC present in these tinctures work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort, providing fast-acting relief from chronic neck pain. Additionally, the topical application of marijuana tinctures allows for targeted treatment directly at the site of pain, making it an effective solution for those seeking natural neck pain relief.
remedy_properties: Cannabinoid Analgesia: The CBD and THC compounds in herbal marijuana tinctures work together to provide rapid relief from chronic neck pain by binding to pain receptors in the body, reducing inflammation and releasing endorphins.

Muscle Relaxation: Herbal marijuana tinctures contain compounds like cannabidiol (CBD) and other terpenes that help relax tense muscles, reducing spasms and stiffness associated with neck pain, allowing for greater range of motion and reduced discomfort.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: The non-psychoactive cannabinoids found in herbal marijuana tinctures, such as CBD, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck and surrounding muscles, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cannabinoids (CBN, CBD, THC): These phytocannabinoids interact with the body's endocannabinoid system to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and relax muscles, providing relief from neck pain.
Terpene (Beta-Caryophyllene): This terpene has been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.

Flavonoid (Quercetin): As an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, quercetin helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the muscles and joints of the neck, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in cannabinoids, particularly THC and CBD, which are responsible for the analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate neck pain.
Leaves: They contain a high concentration of CBD, a non-psychoactive compound that provides pain relief and reduces inflammation in the neck area.
Stems: They are rich in a variety of cannabinoids, including CBG and CBGV, which have been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh cannabis sativa flowers and trim them to release their oils.
Combine the harvested flowers with 500ml of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper lid at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tinctures helps with neck pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on the affected area. The saponins present in burdock root also help to relax the muscles, relieving tension and stiffness. Additionally, burdock's antioxidant properties help to reduce oxidative stress, promoting healthy joint function and reducing inflammation. As a result, herbal burdock tinctures can provide effective relief from neck pain and improve overall flexibility and mobility.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Burdock root's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as lignans and polyphenols, may help reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles and joints, which can alleviate pain.

Antioxidant activity: The high antioxidant content of burdock tincture may help protect the body from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic neck pain conditions like fibromyalgia.

Soothing and calming properties: Burdock's bitter compounds have a calming effect on the nervous system, which may help reduce muscle tension and spasms in the neck and shoulders, leading to relief from pain.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Arctium lappa tinctures help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the neck by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Triterpene saponins: Triterpene saponins present in Arctium lappa tinctures possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which contribute to their effectiveness in relieving neck pain and discomfort.
Polysaccharides: Polysaccharides in Arctium lappa tinctures have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the neck.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate neck pain.
Seeds: The seeds are used for their warming and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help relieve neck pain and stiffness.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce neck pain and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the root of the plant by digging carefully around it with a shovel to avoid damaging it.
Dry the harvested root by spreading it out in a single layer on a wire rack for 1 to 3 weeks.
Chop the dried root into small pieces to increase its surface area for better extraction with a knife.
Steep the chopped root in 70 to 80 proof vodka as a solvent by placing it in a clean glass jar for 2 to 6 weeks.
Strain the tincture by pouring it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar to remove the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which target the underlying causes of discomfort. Curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, has been shown to reduce inflammation and stiffness in the muscles and joints, providing relief from chronic neck strain. Additionally, turmeric's analgesic and antioxidant effects help to soothe and calm irritated tissues, promoting a sense of relaxation and reduced pain perception. As a natural and non-invasive treatment option, herbal turmeric tinctures offer a gentle yet effective way to alleviate neck pain and improve overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Curcuminoids, a group of compounds found in turmeric, have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neck strain or injury.

Corticosteroid-like activity: Turmeric tincture has been shown to exhibit corticosteroid-like activity, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the neck and surrounding tissues, providing relief from pain and stiffness.

Muscle-relaxant properties: The curcuminoids and other bioactive compounds present in turmeric tincture have a natural muscle-relaxant effect, which can help ease muscle tension and spasms in the neck, promoting relaxation and reducing pain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with neck pain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, which are often underlying causes of pain and discomfort in the neck region.
Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound found in Curcuma longa, contributes to its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate neck pain and discomfort.

Bisdemethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC, a phenolic compound with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helps reduce neck pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, which contribute to pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used to make tinctures for neck pain due to their high content of curcumin, a bioactive compound with potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Roots are used to make tinctures for neck pain because they contain a high amount of curcumin and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Buds: Buds are used to make tinctures for neck pain due to their high concentration of curcumin and other active compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relieve pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes from a sunny location after the harvest season begins.
Wash the rhizomes with cold water to remove any dirt or debris then dry them completely.
Chop 250g of the dried rhizomes into small pieces then add 750ml of 95 vodka to a glass jar.
Steep the rhizome mixture in the vodka for 2 weeks in a cool dark place shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container discard the solids and label the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can effectively reduce swelling and ease tension in the muscles. The warming sensation produced by ginger can also help relax the neck muscles and increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and reducing stiffness. Additionally, ginger's analgesic effects can provide relief from sharp pains and discomforts, making it a valuable natural remedy for alleviating neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce swelling and pain in the neck muscles and joints, providing relief from stiffness and discomfort.

Pain-relieving chemicals: Ginger contains chemicals like sesquiterpenes and paradol, which have natural analgesic and anti-pain properties that can help numb or alleviate neck pain, making it easier to move and stretch.

Muscle relaxants: Ginger's active compounds, such as gingerols, also exhibit muscle-relaxing effects, which can help soothe tense muscles in the neck, shoulders, and upper back, reducing spasms and strain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerol: Gingerol is a phenolic compound that helps alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Shogaol: Shogaol, a bioactive compound derived from ginger, may help with neck pain by inhibiting the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body and reducing inflammation.
Curcumenol: Curcumenol, a sesquiterpene compound found in ginger, has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties that may help alleviate neck pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: Roots are used for their ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making them a popular choice for treating neck pain.
Buds: Buds are used due to their high volatile oil content, which is believed to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects that help relieve neck pain.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of the rhizome of zingiber officinale and chop it into small pieces to release its active compounds.
Combine the chopped rhizome with 2 parts of a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin in a clean glass container.
Store the container in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to extract.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids.
Transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica tinctures helps with neck pain because it contains bioactive compounds that possess analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. When taken as a supplement, angelica tincture can help reduce muscle tension and inflammation in the neck area, which are common causes of chronic pain. Additionally, its ability to relax muscles and calm the nervous system can also alleviate stress and anxiety that may contribute to neck tension and discomfort. As a result, many people find relief from their neck pain by incorporating angelica tincture into their daily routine.
remedy_properties: Ant-inflammatory and analgesic properties: Angelica tincture has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, making it effective in relieving neck pain.
Muscle relaxant and sedative properties: The tincture's ability to relax muscles and promote relaxation can help ease tension and stiffness in the neck, reducing the risk of muscle spasms and pain.

Antispasmodic and antirheumatic properties: Angelica's ability to reduce spasms and inflammation in joints and muscles can help alleviate chronic neck pain caused by conditions such as fibromyalgia or arthritis.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds, particularly ferulic acid, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce pain and swelling associated with neck pain.
Furocoumarins (specifically, bergapten): Bergapten has shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing muscle spasms and inflammation.

Coumarins (specifically, angelicine): Angelicine has been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help relax tense muscles in the neck and reduce pain caused by muscle strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with neck pain.
Leaves: They contain anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and relax the muscles in the neck.
Stems: They have been traditionally used to reduce pain and inflammation due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds.
remedy_recipe: Harvest angelica roots and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces to increase the surface area for extraction.
Combine the chopped angelica with 60-80% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1 part plant to 2 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks shaking the jar every few days to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and ease stiffness in the affected area. The capsicum content in bell peppers also stimulates blood flow to the muscles, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms that often contribute to neck pain. Additionally, the antioxidant-rich formula helps to protect against oxidative stress and promotes overall well-being, allowing for a more effective and sustainable relief from neck discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Bell peppers contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which may alleviate neck pain caused by conditions such as arthritis or muscle strain.

Pain-relieving compounds: Bell peppers contain analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds like capsaicin, which may help relieve neck pain and stiffness by reducing prostaglandins (hormone-like substances that cause pain) and blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals.

Vitamin C and collagen synthesis: Bell peppers are rich in vitamin C, which is essential for collagen synthesis, a protein that gives structure to connective tissue. Maintaining healthy collagen levels may help alleviate neck pain caused by degenerative disc disease or osteoporosis.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck area by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the neck muscles, leading to pain relief.
Beta-Carotene: Beta-carotene, a carotenoid, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to protect the neck muscles and joints from damage, reducing pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits, more commonly known as peppers, contain capsaicin, which helps to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck.
Leaves: The leaves contain capsaicin and other compounds that help to ease pain and inflammation, making them a popular choice for neck pain relief.
Seeds: The seeds contain capsaicin and other analgesic compounds that help to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck, making them a popular ingredient in tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh capsicum annuum leaves and 250g of fresh capsicum annuum fruit, ideally during peak ripeness, for tincture preparation.
Chop the fresh materials into small pieces, and combine them with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily, to ensure optimal extraction of the plant's active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter, collecting the liquid and discarding the solids, after the steeping period.
Transfer the tincture to a clean glass bottle, and store it in a cool, dark place, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Masterwort tinctures helps with neck pain because it contains a unique combination of bioactive compounds that work together to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and improve joint mobility. The tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate pressure on the neck and shoulder muscles, while its relaxing effects calm tension and stiffness in the area. Additionally, masterwort's ability to increase blood flow promotes healing and reduces pain perception, providing fast and effective relief from neck pain.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Masterwort tincture contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation in the affected areas, thereby alleviating neck pain caused by inflammation or irritation.

Antioxidant properties: Masterwort's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals and prevent oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic neck pain and stiffness, and also protect against tissue damage from inflammation.

Muscle relaxant properties: The tincture contains compounds that have a relaxing effect on muscles, helping to reduce muscle tension and spasms in the neck and shoulders, thereby providing relief from neck pain caused by strain or overuse.
remedy_constituents: Falcarinol: This sesquiterpene alcohol has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and pain in the neck area by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Falcarinone: Similar to falcarinol, falcarinone is also a sesquiterpene alcohol with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing pain and inflammation associated with neck pain.

Coumarins: Specifically, the coumarins scopoletin and scopoletin glucoside have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: They are used due to their potential pain-relieving properties.
Stems: They are used due to their potential anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried peucedanum ostruthium roots and leaves at peak botanical maturity for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine 100g of chopped plant material with 500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for complete extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with neck pain because its active compounds, particularly absinthin and anabsinthin, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When consumed as a tincture, these compounds are absorbed into the bloodstream and can travel to the affected area, targeting inflammation and discomfort in the neck muscles and joints. This natural relief can help alleviate tension headaches, stiffness, and soreness associated with whiplash, strain, or sprains, promoting relaxation and improved range of motion in the neck and surrounding areas.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate neck pain caused by swelling or irritation in the muscles and joints.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Wormwood contains compounds like absinthin and sesquiterpene lactones that have been shown to possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making it a natural pain reliever for neck pain.
Antispasmodic properties: Wormwood's flavonoids and terpenes help relax muscle spasms and cramps, which can contribute to neck pain caused by tightness or strain in the muscles.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone is a sesquiterpene ketone that has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate neck pain by reducing inflammation and pain perception.
Absinthin: Absinthin is a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the neck, providing relief from discomfort.

Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide is a sesquiterpene with potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation in the neck, thereby providing relief from neck pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with neck pain.
Stems: Utilized for their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from muscle spasms and tension in the neck.
Buds: Employed for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relax tense muscles and reduce pain in the neck area.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature absinthium leaves and stems with scissors or pruning shears in the morning to avoid moisture.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 35-40 degrees celsius for 7-10 days.
Combine 50 grams of the dried plant material with 750 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks with occasional shaking and stirring.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids and store the tincture.

remedy_recipe: Harvest tilia platyphyllos leaves in early morning dew using scissors or pruning shears.
Combine one part dried tilia platyphyllos leaves with two parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for 4-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a separate container.
Transfer the filtered tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool place.

remedy_recipe: Harvest stachys officinalis leaves and stems at dawn, gathering approximately one ounce of fresh plant material.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and combine with 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six to eight weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower tinctures helps with neck pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers and foliage from a trusted source.
Clean the harvested plant material by gently brushing off any dirt or debris and rinsing with distilled water.
Combine 1 part echinacea purpurea plant material with 5 parts 95% ethanol or vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the tincture to mature and extract the beneficial compounds.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tinctures helps with neck pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. When taken orally or applied topically, fennel's active compounds, such as anethole, work to reduce muscle spasms and inflammation in the neck and shoulder region. This relief can help alleviate tension headaches, stiffness, and limited mobility often associated with neck pain. Additionally, fennel's warming properties can promote relaxation, reducing overall stress and promoting a sense of calm, further contributing to its effectiveness in managing neck discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling and discomfort in the muscles and joints associated with neck pain.
Pain-relieving properties: Fennel's essential oils, including anethole and fenchone, have analgesic and anti-nociceptive effects, providing natural relief from neck pain and stiffness.

Muscle relaxant properties: Fennel's tincture has been shown to relax muscle spasms and cramps, helping to alleviate tension and discomfort in the neck and shoulder muscles that can contribute to chronic neck pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are used to make medicinal tinctures because they contain a high concentration of natural analgesics and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help relieve neck pain.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are used to create medicinal tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation, relax muscles, and ease tension in the neck and shoulder region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves in early morning dew for optimal potency.
Wash the harvested leaves with pure water to remove dirt and debris then chop into small pieces.
Combine chopped foeniculum vulgare leaves with 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar at a ratio of 1 part herb to 4 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain and filter the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids then store.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with neck pain because it contains mucilage, a soothing gel-like substance that coats and protects the delicate tissues in the neck, reducing inflammation and discomfort. As a natural anti-inflammatory, marshmallow tincture also reduces swelling and pain caused by sprains or strains in the neck muscles. Additionally, its gentle demulcent properties help to calm irritated nerves, promoting relaxation and relieving tension that can contribute to chronic neck pain.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps to soothe and calm irritated tissues in the body, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with neck pain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Marshmallow tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the neck, making it an effective natural remedy for conditions like cervical radiculitis or whiplash.

Soothing demulcent action: The demulcent properties of marshmallow tincture create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes and skin, providing relief from dryness, irritation, and discomfort associated with neck strain or injury.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick and protective substance that can help soothe and calm irritated joints and muscles in the neck.
Barks: The barks contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and relieve pain in the neck.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Cleanse the marigold flowers by rinsing them gently in cold water and patting dry.
Combine 100 grams of the clean marigold flowers with 500 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days at room temperature shaking daily before straining.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb tinctures helps with neck pain because of its unique properties that stimulate digestion and relieve muscle tension. The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds work to reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected area, while its gentle warming sensation soothes stiff muscles and joints. As a natural relaxant, Chinese rhubarb also helps calm the mind and body, promoting deeper relaxation and reducing stress-induced neck pain.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Chinese Rhubarb tincture helps to reduce inflammation and swelling in the neck muscles, which can contribute to neck pain.
Antispasmodic properties: The tincture's ability to relax muscle spasms can provide relief from tension headaches and neck stiffness associated with cervical strain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Chinese Rhubarb's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as anthraquinones, help to reduce pain and discomfort caused by inflammation in the neck and surrounding joints.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anthraquinones, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve muscle spasms in the neck.
Rhyzomes: Contain astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing swelling and pain in the cervical region.
remedy_recipe: Harvest rheum palmatum roots and cut them into small pieces ensuring they are completely dry.
Combine the dried root pieces with high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar filling it about 3 quarters full.
Store the jar in a cool dark place allowing the mixture to infuse for 6-8 weeks shaking occasionally daily.
After infusion filter the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place using it within 2 years as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with neck pain because it has a unique ability to regulate hormonal balance, particularly progesterone levels. When used topically or orally, chaste tree tincture can help alleviate tension and stress in the neck and shoulder muscles, leading to relief from chronic pain. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and inflammation in the affected area, providing further comfort and relaxation. As a natural remedy, chaste tree tincture offers a gentle yet effective approach to managing neck pain and promoting overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Hormonal regulation: Chasteberry, or Vitex agnus-castus, has been shown to balance hormonal fluctuations, which can contribute to neck pain and stiffness caused by hormonal imbalances.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and iridoid glycosides present in chaste tree tinctures have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with neck strain or arthritis.

Muscle relaxant activity: Chasteberry has been found to possess muscle relaxant properties, which can help ease muscle tension and spasms in the neck and shoulder region, reducing pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chaste tree are used to make tinctures for neck pain because they contain a rich concentration of essential oils and compounds that help reduce inflammation and relax muscle tension.
Seeds: Chaste tree seeds are used in tincture production due to their ability to relieve muscle spasms and stiffness, making them effective against chronic neck pain caused by muscle strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried vitex agnus castus berries in the correct quantity for the recipe.
Combine the berries with vodka or ethanol at a ratio of 1 part plant material to 4 parts solvent.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid for four to six weeks to allow for extraction.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid and label it clearly.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for neck pain are concentrated liquid extracts made from plants, herbs, and botanicals that have natural anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. These tinctures help alleviate neck pain by reducing muscle spasms, inflammation, and tension in the neck and shoulder regions. Examples of herbal tinctures that provide relief include willow bark, which contains salicylic acid similar to aspirin, and turmeric, with its powerful anti-inflammatory compound curcumin. By incorporating these tinctures into daily routines, individuals can experience improved mobility, reduced pain and stiffness, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to enjoy a more active and comfortable lifestyle.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neck pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are conditions such as arthritis, fibromyalgia, and sciatica. Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, like willow bark, ginger, and devil's claw, may help alleviate symptoms of these conditions. Additionally, tinctures featuring relaxing and pain-relieving herbs like valerian, passionflower, and skullcap can address related issues like insomnia, anxiety, and muscle tension.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with neck pain is a blend of Turmeric, Willow Bark, and Ginger. Turmeric contains anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain, while Willow Bark's salicin helps to ease muscle tension and discomfort. Ginger adds its warming and anti-inflammatory properties to the mix, promoting circulation and relaxation. Taking 10-20 drops of each tincture 2-3 times a day can provide relief from neck pain and stiffness, allowing for a more comfortable and active lifestyle.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by The Cochrane database of systematic reviews, Chinese herbal medicines have shown efficacy in treating chronic neck pain with radicular signs or symptoms. The study, which included four randomized controlled trials with a total of 1,110 participants, found that three different herbal formulas and one topical herbal medicine provided pain relief when compared to placebo or conventional medications. Specifically, the oral herbal formula of Huangqi and Compound Qishe Tablets, as well as the topical herbal medicine, Compound Extractum Nucis Vomicae, were found to relieve pain better than their respective controls, indicating that herbal tinctures may be a useful adjunctive treatment for neck pain.
